title: Helichrysum retortum
intro: Helichrysum retortum, commonly known as the Cape goldensal or yellow mountain daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Helichrysum retortum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Helichrysum retortum is commonly prepared as a tincture, essential oil, or dried flower infusion, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Helichrysum retortum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Helichrysum, which consists of daisy-like plants native to the Mediterranean region. Helichrysum retortum is specifically classified within the subfamily Gnaphalioideae, a group of daisy-like plants with small, white or yellow flowers. The plant's classification is further supported by its characteristics, such as its hairy leaves and stems, and its small, composite flowers. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Helichrysum retortum from other related species within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Helichrysum retortum, also known as the Cape golden aster, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. Its extracts are also believed to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections. Additionally, Helichrysum retortum is thought to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. However, more research is needed to confirm its medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

constituents: Helichrysum retortum contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These constituents are thought to contribute to the plant's potential health benefits and antimicrobial properties.
